summary: The Sprint 8 module split significantly improves maintainability — analytics.py was a 700+ line monolith and the new split into domain-specific modules (metrics, history, fx) is clean and follows project conventions. The analytics_utils.py shared service pattern is correct. New TypeScript section components are well-typed with explicit interfaces. The analytics.py compatibility shim using # noqa: F401 is a pragmatic backward-compat solution. Two areas need attention: analytics_metrics.py at 383 lines approaches the 400-line guideline, and there is duplicated date_ticker_map construction logic across analytics_metrics.py and analytics_history.py that should eventually be extracted.

findings.title: Duplicated date_ticker_map construction across analytics modules
findings.detail: Both analytics_metrics.py and analytics_history.py contain identical code to build a dict[str, dict[str, float]] from price snapshots. This pattern also appears in get_monthly_returns. Any bug fix to this logic requires updates in 3+ places.
findings.fix: Extract to analytics_utils.py: def build_date_ticker_map(snapshots: Sequence[PriceSnapshot]) -> dict[str, dict[str, float]]: ...

findings.title: analytics_metrics.py approaches 400-line guideline
findings.detail: The file is 383 lines. Project convention is 200-400 lines typical, 800 max. It is within bounds but worth noting for the next sprint — get_metrics alone is 150 lines.
findings.fix: Consider extracting _build_portfolio_values() helper to reduce get_metrics body size in a future sprint.

findings.title: analytics.py shim retains large import block from original module
findings.detail: The analytics.py shim still imports many models and services (Optional, date, Holding, Portfolio, etc.) that are no longer used in that file — they were needed by the original endpoints which are now in the split modules. These unused imports were removed by ruff --fix, but the shim may cause confusion about what still lives in analytics.py vs the new modules.
findings.fix: Add a module docstring to analytics.py clearly stating: 'This module is a backward-compatibility shim. All endpoints have moved to analytics_metrics.py, analytics_history.py, analytics_fx.py.'

findings.title: Three new analytics router files register the same prefix
findings.detail: All three new routers use prefix='/analytics'. While FastAPI supports this, it means the prefix is repeated in 3 places. If the prefix changes, 3 files need updating.
findings.fix: This is acceptable given FastAPI's router composition model. Document in main.py with a comment grouping the three analytics routers together.
